medical_history.chief_complaint: Left tentorial hemorrhage; cerebellar ICH with tracking into the brainstem.
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient denies prior medical history. Sudden onset slurred speech and right arm numbness experienced while socializing at a bar. Taken to OSH where CT showed left IPH. Transferred for further evaluation. Denies headache; positive for nausea and dizziness. Reports numbness in right arm and left face.
medical_history.past_medical_history: S/p tonsillectomy at age Not specified.

general_examination.notable_systemic_findings: General: WD/WN, comfortable, NAD. HEENT: normocephalic, atraumatic. Neck: Supple. Extremities: Warm and well-perfused.

specialty_examination.neurological: Mental status: Awake and alert, cooperative with exam, normal affect; mild inattention. Orientation: Oriented to person, place, and date. Language: Thickened speech with good comprehension and repetition. Cranial Nerves: Decreased movement in lateral gaze of left eye, mild left ptosis, slight nasolabial fold flattening on left, decreased V1-V3 sensation. Tongue deviates to left. Motor: Mild right pronator drift, full power throughout. Sensation: Decreased to light touch on right arm and leg. Coordination: Positive dysmetria bilaterally.
specialty_examination.other: Physical examination on discharge noted normal mental status, cranial nerves: physiologic anisocoria, left nerve palsy, and more.
